title: Yogyakarta 7-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Yogyakarta 7-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Yogyakarta, Indonesia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Borobudur Temple and Prambanan Temple. Prices from $1931 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Yogyakarta, Indonesia — Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 29-31°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March. May to September — the dry season, ideal for sunrise temple visits and volcano trekking. This itinerary blends Borobudur Temple, Prambanan Temple, Malioboro Street with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Borobudur Temple, Prambanan Temple, Malioboro Street
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Yogyakarta
itinerary.description: Land at Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Borobudur Temple — The world's largest Buddhist temple, best at sunrise. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Yogyakarta: Prambanan Temple & Taman Sari Water Castle
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Prambanan Temple — Towering 9th-century Hindu temple complex. In the afternoon, continue to Taman Sari Water Castle — Former royal garden and bathing complex of the Sultanate. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Yogyakarta: Kraton (Sultan's Palace) & Mount Merapi jeep tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Kraton (Sultan's Palace) — Still-functioning royal palace of the Yogyakarta Sultanate. In the afternoon, continue to Mount Merapi jeep tour — Off-road tour across the slopes of Indonesia's most active volcano. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Borobudur and Prambanan temples
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Borobudur and Prambanan temples, a popular side trip from Yogyakarta. Return to Yogyakarta in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Borobudur and Prambanan temples

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Tebing Breksi limestone quarry viewpoint — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Yogyakarta: Malioboro Street & Kotagede silver district
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Malioboro Street — Yogyakarta's main shopping and street-food thoroughfare. In the afternoon, continue to Kotagede silver district — Historic neighborhood known for silver craftsmanship. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Borobudur Temple or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to YIA for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book the Borobudur sunrise tour in advance — it requires a separate, limited-capacity ticket beyond standard entry.
Hire a driver for a full-day Borobudur, Prambanan, and Merapi combination — it's efficient and affordable.
Try gudeg, Yogyakarta's signature sweet jackfruit stew, at a well-reviewed local warung.
Malioboro Street is best explored in the evening when it's liveliest and cooler.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable walking shoes for temple complexes
A hat and sun protection for the Borobudur sunrise climb
thingsToDo: Becak (rickshaw) ride through the old town
thingsToAvoid: Climbing Borobudur or Prambanan without sun protection — both sites are largely shadeless.
Skipping a guide at the temples — the carved reliefs and history reward context.
Underestimating distances between Yogyakarta city and Borobudur/Merapi — allow a full day for both.
topRestaurants: Gudeg Yu Djum — Traditional gudeg (jackfruit stew) ($)
House of Raminten — Javanese, quirky decor ($$)
Bale Raos — Royal Kraton-recipe Javanese cuisine ($$)
instagramSpots: Borobudur at sunrise with mist over the hills
familyActivities: Becak (rickshaw) ride through the old town
luxuryExperiences: Private sunrise Borobudur tour with a premium viewpoint
Suite stay at a resort near the temples
Private guided Kraton and Taman Sari tour
honeymoonExperiences: Private dinner with a traditional Javanese dance performance
localEtiquette: Dress modestly when visiting temples — shoulders and knees covered, a sarong is often provided.
Remove shoes before entering homes and some temple areas.
Yogyakarta is considered Java's cultural heart — respect for traditional arts (batik, gamelan, wayang puppetry) runs deep.
localTransportGuide: Central Yogyakarta is compact and walkable or cyclable; becak (pedicab) and andong (horse cart) rides are traditional ways to cover the old town. For Borobudur, Prambanan, or Merapi, most visitors book a driver or organized tour, since sites are spread across the surrounding region.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September — the dry season, ideal for sunrise temple visits and volcano trekking.
weather: Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 29-31°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March.
visaInformation: Indonesia offers visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, valid for 30 days and extendable once.
averageFlightTime: ~11.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Yogyakarta?
faqs.answer: Indonesia offers visa-on-arrival for most nationalities including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, valid for 30 days and extendable once.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Yogyakarta?
faqs.answer: May to September — the dry season, ideal for sunrise temple visits and volcano trekking. Tropical climate — warm year-round (highs around 29-31°C), with a wetter monsoon season roughly November to March.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Yogyakarta safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Yogyakarta is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Yogyakarta?
faqs.answer: The local currency is IDR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Ethan Kowalski
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Malioboro Street and Borobudur Temple. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Lukas Patel
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Yogyakarta is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Prambanan Temple. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Priya Larsen
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Mount Merapi jeep tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Botha
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Mount Merapi jeep tour alone was worth the trip, but combined with Kotagede silver district this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Emma Johnson
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Our trip to Yogyakarta was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Mount Merapi jeep tour was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
